generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VedderESC : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Vedder Electric Speed Controller translating desired velocity commands into motor voltage signals for the vehicle's drive‑by‑wire architecture."
]
instance ChassisCommandLoop : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Nested real‑time command loop governing steering angle and longitudinal speed using feedback from the ESC and vehicle sensors."
]
instance ROS2Network : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "ROS2 communication middleware running over Wi‑Fi, enabling low‑latency command dissemination between the Digital Twin executors and physical actuators."
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoUST10LxLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST10LX LiDAR supplying 360° laser scans up to 20 m at 30 Hz."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudPacket
]
instance IntelDepthSenseD435iCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel DepthSense D435i RGB‑D camera delivering 2 m depth imagery with 1 mm resolution at 30 Hz."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraFramePacket
]
instance WheelEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Wheel encoder providing rotational velocity feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OdometryPacket
]
instance IMU : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Inertial Measurement Unit supplying acceleration and angular rate data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OdometryPacket
]
instance GPS : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "GPS unit providing globally referenced positional data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OdometryPacket
]

// C4: Physical‑to‑Virtual Interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudPacket : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LxLiDAR
]
instance DepthCameraFramePacket : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDepthSenseD435iCamera
]
instance OdometryPacket : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom WheelEncoder, IMU, GPS
]
instance V2TVehicleTelemetryPacket : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LxLiDAR
]
instance V2ITrafficTelemetryPacket : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDepthSenseD435iCamera
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated LiDAR point cloud frames stored per SQL frame, including ego pose, velocity, and heading."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudPacket
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]
instance DepthCameraImageData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "RGB‑D image frames derived from the Intel DepthSense D435i camera."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthCameraFramePacket
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]
instance OdometryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Globally referenced odometry stream fusing wheel encoder, IMU, and GPS data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OdometryPacket
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]
instance V2VTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle‑to‑vehicle telemetry capturing lane position, speed, and lead‑vehicle proximity at 10 Hz."
    DTDFVocab:fromData V2TVehicleTelemetryPacket
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]
instance V2ITelemetryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle‑to‑infrastructure telemetry stream at 10 Hz."
    DTDFVocab:fromData V2ITrafficTelemetryPacket
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]
instance VehicleTracksData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Predicted object tracks generated by DBSCAN clustering followed by Kalman filtering."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler, ComputationOffloadingEnabler
]
instance ACCTrajectoryPredictionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Projected driving path computed by the path planning module."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingEnabler
]
instance RiskAssessmentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Bayesian risk estimate used for decision support."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingEnabler
]
instance ProjectedPathData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Projected path visualized by the 3D Rendering FE."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingEnabler
]
instance DesiredSpeedCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Desired speed command generated by the ACC logic."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingEnabler
]
instance EmergencyBrakeCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Emergency braking command issued via remote management."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingEnabler
]

// Models
instance DBSCANClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "DBSCAN clustering algorithm segmenting raw LiDAR point clouds into discrete obstacles."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]
instance KalmanFilterTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kalman filter predicting and updating each obstacle’s state over time."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]
instance BayesianRiskEstimatorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Bayesian risk estimator generating confidence scores for ACC decisions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingEnabler
]
instance PathPlanningModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Path planning module generating projected driving paths."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingEnabler
]
instance CollisionAvoidanceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Collision avoidance logic processing tracking data for safe maneuvers."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data Translation entity performing unit conversion, timestamp alignment, DBSCAN clustering, and Kalman filtering."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]
instance ComputationOffloadingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Edge compute node executing trajectory prediction and risk assessment on Dockerised Python framework."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]
instance RemoteControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Remote management enabler handling low‑end‑to‑end latency communication for ACC control commands."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real‑time UI exposing vehicle state, projected path, and object tracks supports viewing, saving, and reporting."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight
]
instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloaded ACC logic computing lead vehicle identification, desired speed, and risk assessment sends remote speed commands."
    DTDFVocab:provides BayesianRiskInsight, ProjectedPathInsight, ACCDecisionInsight, SendSpeedCommandAction
]
instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Operator interface via React Web UI allowing enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, and issue emergency braking."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction, SetDesiredSpeedAction, EmergencyBrakeAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight reflecting real‑time vehicle state and projected driving path."
]
instance BayesianRiskInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing Bayesian risk estimate and confidence scores for ACC decisions."
]
instance ProjectedPathInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight visualizing the projected driving path for the vehicle."
]
instance ACCDecisionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight reporting ACC decision outputs including desired speed."
]
instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Emergency braking command issued remotely via UI."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to enable or disable Adaptive Cruise Control."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to set desired speed for ACC."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance SendSpeedCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to send remotely computed desired speed command to the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "High‑level directives (e.g., target speed or emergency‑braking request) are published by the Digital Twin on a ROS 2 topic and captured by the Actuation FE. This FE validates the command payload against the ISO 23247 schema, translates it into low‑level actuator signals (motor voltage commands, steering servo positions), and broadcasts the command over a UDP/Wi‑Fi channel that the vehicle’s on‑board controller subscribes to. The controller executes the actuator update, publishes a confirmation status on a separate feedback topic, and the DT verifies execution confirmation. If confirmation is absent or mismatched, conflict‑resolution logic triggers an emergency‑brake sequence. Authentication and integrity checks, such as per‑message MACs and session tokens, are applied to the UDP packets to mitigate tampering."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Shared notion of time across all devices is mandatory for computation offloading; clock synchronization accuracy must be ≤ 100 ms, as required by the system’s hardware limitations, even though many industry standards demand tighter bounds for safety‑critical automotive applications. The system therefore synchronises clocks between the physical and digital twin entities via ROS 2 timekeeping mechanisms; the documentation does not specify an exact frequency for these synchronization events."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The overall orchestration follows the ISO 23247 Reference Architecture and is realized through a three‑entity decomposition: (1) Digital Twin Entity—encapsulates data management and control mechanisms; (2) Physical Twin Entity—manages data pre‑processing, actuation, and device communication, and hosts general automotive algorithms such as point‑cloud clustering and tracking; (3) User Entity—provides user‑facing functionality via a User Interface FE split into a 3D Visualization and a Control Panel. Each Functional Entity (FE) is implemented as a dedicated ROS 2 package, containing one or more ROS 2 nodes that execute the required functionality. These packages are independently deployed on distinct hardware nodes, enabling distributed execution across the vehicle’s computational grid. The architecture accommodates deviations from ISO 23247 by incorporating the Physical Twin Entity as a distinct entity representing the system’s inherent resources, thereby influencing ownership and engineering processes of the Digital Twin System (DTS)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Communication between application components occurs over a ROS2 middleware layer; nodes publish/subscribe within a ROS2 network that leverages UDP over the vehicle’s Wi‑Fi connection for inter‑entity communication. The architecture supports real‑time data feeds but the specifications for bandwidth, latency, reliability, redundancy, and security protocols are not described in the document."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Compliance with ISO 23247 – Digital twin framework for manufacturing. The system adopts an entity-based reference architecture defined in Part 2 of the standard, comprising the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity. Each entity contains Functional Entities (FEs) for tasks such as data collection, device control, digital representation, and user interface. The standard permits choice of communication middleware (e.g., OPC UA), whereas the implementation selected for this system uses a ROS 2 network to achieve interoperability and data exchange with manufacturing equipment and user applications."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin’s Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) subsystem is governed by ISO 15622:2018, which specifies performance requirements and test procedures for intelligent transport systems. The system’s architecture implements ACC as per the guidelines in ISO 15622, ensuring compliance with the defined acceptance tests and safety performance metrics. While the documents explicitly reference that the system respects the safety‑critical nature of automotive control, no formal safety‑analysis standard (e.g., ISO 26262) is cited, and details on failure‑mode handling, fault‑tolerant design, or safety assurance processes are not provided. Likewise, cybersecurity measures are absent from the referenced specifications; access control, authentication mechanisms, and threat‑mitigation strategies are not specified. Consequently, it can be inferred that the Digital Twin relies on the safety and performance criteria outlined in ISO 15622, but further security and safety‑critical system details would need to be defined separately."
]
